name: Proving the "Unseen" Injury: A Guide to Carpal Tunnel and RSI Claims in Utah
description: Proving the "Unseen" Injury: A Guide to Carpal Tunnel and RSI Claims in Utah - Salt Lake City

step.name: Step 1
step.text: Unlike a slip and fall, a repetitive stress injury (RSI) like carpal tunnel or tendonitis creeps up slowly. This makes the "How-To" of filing a claim much more complex. Because there isn't one specific "accident date," the insurance company will try to claim your injury is from your lifestyle or hobbies. Here is how to build a winning RSI claim.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Think about your workday. Do you perform the same motion more than 30 times a minute? Do you use vibrating tools? Do you spend 6+ hours typing or scanning? Document these tasks. You need to be able to explain to a doctor exactly how many times a day you perform the offending motion.

step.name: Step 5
step.text: The moment you feel numbness, tingling, or "pins and needles," tell your employer. You don't need a diagnosis yet to report "work-related symptoms." This starts the clock and protects you from the 30-day reporting denial.

step.name: Step 7
step.text: You need more than just a "sore wrist." You need objective testing. Ask your doctor about:

step.name: Step 8
step.text: - **EMG (Electromyography):** Tests the health of muscles and the nerve cells that control them.

step.name: Step 9
step.text: - **Nerve Conduction Velocity (NCV) Test:** Measures how fast electrical impulses move through your nerves.

step.name: Step 10
step.text: If these tests show damage, and you have a high-repetition job, it becomes very difficult for an insurance company to deny your claim.

step.name: Step 12
step.text: The insurer will search your medical history for any mention of arthritis or previous wrist pain. Do not let this scare you. Under the law, if your work "lit up" or worsened a pre-existing condition, you are still covered.

step.name: Step 14
step.text: As part of your claim, you can request that a specialist look at your workspace. Sometimes, a simple change in desk height or a new tool can prevent further injury, but if the damage is done, this report serves as evidence that your workspace was poorly designed for your body.

step.name: Step 16
step.text: RSI cases are the most frequently denied workers' comp claims. Because they rely so heavily on medical nuance, having an attorney like those at Just Work Comp Law is often the only way to get a denial overturned. We know the doctors, we know the defense tactics, and we know how to prove that your job—not your life—caused your pain.
